channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Recurring litter and public waste provision near Tesco / Mossbourne Riverside, 5th July
item.description:
Overflowing bin and litter on the ground by Tesco / Tandy Place, near Mossbourne Riverside Academy, following Hackney Bridge event footfall. Recurring issue. Please link to ME 46457149 regarding bin provision and cleansing capacity.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tandy Place, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A large hole and deep crack on a very recently resurfaced road, 5th July
item.description:
Obviously a pop hole isn't great.Use anyway, but this one is very strange. It made me worry weather, there is a sinkhole below.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 112, Tressillian Road, Brockley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Motor Bike, 5th July
item.description:
Abandoned and vandalised motorbike left in parking area of Block A (1-30) Arnold Estate, SE1 2DL. Please remove.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 74, Jamaica Road, Bermondsey, Greater London

item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Over flowing bin dumped in the alleyway between 265 and 277 Upper Wickham Lane,DA16 3AP . .Fly-tipping happening constantly despite the nearby CCTV Pole., 5th July
item.description:
Over flowing bins dumped in the alleyway between 265 and 277 Upper Wickham Lane,DA16 3AP . .Fly-tipping happening constantly despite the nearby CCTV Pole.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper Wickham Lane, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Raw meat left in overflowing playground bin, 5th July
item.description:
Residents use this as their private waste bin. Raw meat was left overnight, foxes went through it and it's now on the playground. Unacceptable behaviour, this will now be attracting foxes and rats. Please find and fine the residents doing this.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ambleside Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Nw6 5NR, 5th July
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam I am writing to report a pedestrian passage that is consistently covered with rubbish every day. The area is dirty, neglected, and has become unpleasant and intimidating to walk through. The new gate makes it even worse as more rubbish is thrown out there. The situation is made worse by the lack of adequate lighting and the absence of CCTV, which makes the passage feel unsafe, particularly during the evening and at night. I respectfully request an urgent intervention to: - Remove the accumulated rubbish and ensure regular cleaning. - Improve the lighting. - Assess the installation of CCTV to improve safety and deter antisocial behaviour. Please treat this as a matter of urgency and let me know what actions will be taken and the expected timeframe for addressing these issues. Thank you for your prompt attention. Yours faithfully

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Springfield Lane, Kilburn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Builders rubble and bin bags along pavement of Cadiz Street

item.title: Fly tipping, 5th July

Builders materials (again)

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tudor Grove, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog fouling, 5th July
item.description:
This was a problem which was reported on 27th April 2026 and we received a response on 14th May 2026. Whilst I appreciate some action was taken to prevent this, the problem continues and is getting worse. The entire pathway from our front door to the fire exit is blocked by dog fouling. Walking past the outside of the building it can also be smelt. In our previous complaint, we also highlighted that it is not just the dog fouling but the issue of the owners themselves. They have no control over the dog and allow it to run around off lead around the courtyard. It is a large breed which charges at anyone who comes outside. The dog will also jump onto you, nearly knocking people over. It is not suitable to be around children as it could cause serious injury by knocking them onto the hard concrete. When the dog jumps onto residents, the owners make no attempts to stop the dog or remove it from the resident. This also prevents an issue with entering and exiting the courtyard as the dog could easily be accidentally let out and get harmed/ harm someone due to having no recall. The dog needs to be on a lead at all times as it poses a safety risk. This was not addressed in the response to our complaint. The residents at Flat 9 Piper Court have clearly not taking notice of the letter which has been sent to them by the Council and continue to allow the dog to foul all over the communal courtyard. It is a safety hazard and an issue which needs to be addressed more robustly. Please can this be looked into and a response provided. I have been only able to submit 3 photos but it is the entire path which is now covered in dog faeces. Since the last complaint, I have seen the residents of Flat 9 outside with the dog repeatedly allowing it to fouling and not picking it up. I know it to be them who are causing this issue.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bowyer Place, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Constant dumping of rubbish, 5th July
item.description:
Whoever is doing the construction work on the shop on the corner is leaving mess and rubbish outside constantly. They are also working until 1am every night disturbing the street.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mayfield Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London

item.title: Constant ongoing fly tipping!, 5th July
item.description:
Why is this resident still allowed to DUMP their garden waste over their fence? Reported several times but council don’t do a thing! 9 forsyte close Large amounts of garden waste in the wood land!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Forsyte Crescent, Upper Norwood, Greater London

item.title: There is a dead fox alongside the River Shuttle that backs directly behind my house, 5th July
item.description:
This is not a pothole it’s a dead fox but it does not give me the option to state this.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 28, Albany Close, Blendon, Greater London

item.title: Large and uneven area of soil in the middle of a pavement, 5th July
item.description:
There is a large and very uneven section of The pavement outside number 77/79 Henshaw Street that is unfinished since the removal of a tree over a year ago. This is a significant Hazard and the pavement needs to be finished.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Henshaw Street, Walworth, Greater London
